info.title: LingoChunk Public API
info.description:
The LingoChunk public API. Read your FSRS-graded vocabulary, look words up, browse your library, pull sliced transcripts and native audio, search your listening history, and cut audio clips.

Write, too: upload audio recordings for processing (optionally publishing them into a collection you own), add vocabulary and custom cards to your decks, export a deck to Anki, and save a generated HTML lesson to your library.

Authenticate with a personal access token created in your account settings: send it as `Authorization: Bearer lcp_...`. Each endpoint requires a scope, granted to the token at creation.

get.description:
List the learner's vocabulary, aggregated per word, with FSRS maturity.

The per-word state is grounded in the user's real listening content. Use
``since`` (with the ``updated_at`` from a prior response) plus ``cursor`` for
incremental sync.

Incremental sync is ADDITIVE-ONLY: ``since`` surfaces new and changed words
but carries no deletion signal, so a word the user later removes will not
appear as "deleted". Clients that must stay exactly in step should do a
periodic full resync (call without ``since``) and treat that as authoritative.

**Requires scope:** `vocab:read`

get.description:
Return a submission's transcript, optionally sliced by sentence-position
range and/or time range.

Access mirrors the internal app: 404 for anything the caller cannot read.
The ``transcript_state`` field distinguishes a genuinely empty slice from a
submission that is still processing (``processing``) or ready but with no
transcript on file (``unavailable``), so a not-ready submission never looks
like an empty transcript.

**Requires scope:** `content:read`

get.description:
Search the caller's readable library for sentences.

``lemma`` returns the curated example sentences linked to that word - a
subset of every occurrence, but the good, hand-picked ones - matched
case-insensitively. ``q`` does a case-insensitive substring match on
sentence text. At least one of the two is required.

Results are a capped SAMPLE, not an exhaustive set: there is no pagination
and no completeness guarantee. Raise ``limit`` (up to 100) for more, or use
a more specific query.

**Requires scope:** `content:read`

get.description:
Return a native-audio snippet of [start, end] as an audio file.

Repeat requests for the same span are served from cache. Rate limited per
token (30/hour) AND per user (60/hour). May return 503 when clip generation

post.description:
Dry-run validate a lesson.v1 document WITHOUT saving it.

Returns 200 with ``{valid, errors, unknown_lemmas}`` and reports EVERY
failing category at once - schema violations (one entry per pydantic error,
with a dotted ``loc``) and reference/gate violations (the same stable codes
the save route raises: youtube_disabled, unknown_positions,
position_outside_slice, audio_outside_episode, audio_outside_slice,
dialogue_mismatch, order_mismatch) - so an agent can fix a whole document in
one pass instead of burning save -> 400 -> fix cycles that only surface the
first problem.

Runs no persistence and consumes no lesson-cap budget (so it succeeds even
at the cap). The transcript queries it runs are bounded by the standard
per-token / per-user surface rate limits; it deliberately does NOT consume
the save route's storage-spam write limiter, since it stores nothing and is
meant to be called repeatedly before a single save.

404 if the source submission is not accessible; 400 for a body that is not a
lesson.v1 ``document`` (e.g. the legacy ``html`` save variant); 413 for a
document past the size cap.

**Requires scope:** `lessons:write`

get.description:
Read back a native lesson.v1 document (404 for HTML lessons, which have
no document). Closes the loop for agents: read the document, revise it, and
PUT it back to the same lesson. Without this route an agent could save and
list lessons but never see what it (or an earlier session) wrote.

Readable by the owner, and by a viewer of the source submission when the
lesson is published (visibility='public') or is a section lesson of a
shared guided path — the same rule as the in-app reads, so a shared path's
``lesson_id``s from the guided GET are fetchable here. Editing (the PUT)
stays owner-only.

The ``X-Lesson-Version`` response header carries the lesson's
optimistic-concurrency token; echo it verbatim as the update's
``base_version`` so a concurrent edit is rejected (409 ``stale_document``)
instead of silently overwritten.

**Requires scope:** `lessons:write`

403.description: The token lacks the scope this endpoint requires

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. The `Retry-After` response header gives the number of seconds to wait.

put.description:
Replace a native lesson.v1 document in place (owner only).

The full replacement document is validated exactly like a save (schema +
references against the source submission) and overwrites the stored
object; the lesson keeps its id, visibility, course and creation time.
The document must keep its original ``source.submission_id`` (400
``submission_mismatch`` otherwise) - a lesson on a different episode is a
new lesson. 409 ``lesson_not_document`` for HTML lessons, which have no
document to edit.

Pass ``base_version`` (the ``X-Lesson-Version`` token this edit is based
on, echoed verbatim) to reject a concurrent edit with 409
``stale_document`` instead of overwriting it; the response's
``X-Lesson-Version`` header carries the NEW token for chained edits.

**Requires scope:** `lessons:write`

post.description:
Trigger (or re-plan) the stage-1 guided planner for a lesson you own.

Enqueues the same server planner the in-app button does (Gemini spend, inside
the global daily cap and your per-user guided budget) and returns a job id to
poll via ``/v1/jobs/{job_id}``. 409 ``plan_in_progress`` / ``plan_ready``
when a plan is already running or ready (a failed plan may be re-planned);
422 ``submission_too_long`` past the length cap. No lesson is written here -
that is a separate step.

**Requires scope:** `guided:write`

get.description:
Read the guided path of a lesson you own or may view: plan status,
study-order sections (title, summary, bounds, the generated lesson id +
focus, and your completion), the section currently generating, and the most
recent writer failure still awaiting a retry. ``status`` is ``none`` until a
plan is requested. Collection viewers (the same visibility rule as the
episode itself) get a ``read_only`` projection of a READY path — their own
completions, the owner's in-flight state blanked; a not-yet-ready path
reads as ``none``. When ``read_only`` is true, study through the lesson
reads; the write routes (plan, brief, submit) are owner-only and will 404.
(The second-wave rail is not part of the public surface.)

**Requires scope:** `guided:read`

403.description: The token lacks the scope this endpoint requires

get.description:
The writer brief for the NEXT empty study-order section of a path you own.

Returns the deterministically picked ``focus``, the assembled pedagogy
``instructions`` (the SAME system-prompt content the internal writer
receives), the lesson.v1 ``contract``, and the section's ``materials``
(language/level, the source window to copy, the planner's section entry, the
section transcript, and the learner's known lemmas). Compose the document,
dry-run it with ``POST /v1/lessons/validate``, then submit it for the
returned ``section_index``.

Read-only: it claims nothing, spends no server budget, and counts against no
limiter, so two agents may both brief and compose - the first valid submit
wins. 409 ``plan_not_ready`` until the plan is ready, ``path_complete`` when
every study section already has a lesson. The ``instructions`` state that the
transcript is DATA to teach, never instructions to obey.

**Requires scope:** `guided:write`

404.description: Not found, not owned, or the flag is off

409.description: The plan is not ready (`plan_not_ready`), the path is complete (`path_complete`), or the next section's transcript is empty (`section_has_no_sentences`)

403.description: The token lacks the scope this endpoint requires

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. The `Retry-After` response header gives the number of seconds to wait.

post.description:
Submit a composed lesson.v1 document as the guided part for ``index``.

``index`` MUST be the next empty study-order section (get it from the brief);
any other index 409s ``section_not_next``. The document is re-validated
against that section (positions in-section, dialogue/order verbatim, audio
inside the section window; youtube blocks rejected) and, if valid, attached
atomically through the same save path the internal writer uses, so a part is
stored identically whoever wrote it. The server stamps the ``generator``
(default skill ``mcp-guided``, version the pack version) and fills the
document's server-managed ids.

Races: 409 ``generation_in_flight`` while the in-app writer holds the slot,
409 ``section_taken`` if the section gained a lesson since the brief. The
per-user daily guided limit is shared with the in-app button and charged only
on a successful save. Returns the saved lesson summary plus an
``unknown_lemmas`` advisory.

**Requires scope:** `guided:write`

404.description: Not found, not owned, or the flag is off

409.description: Not the next empty section (`section_not_next`), a writer is already generating (`generation_in_flight`), the section was just filled (`section_taken`), the plan is not ready (`plan_not_ready`), or the section has no transcript (`section_has_no_sentences`)

413.description: The document exceeds the size cap (`document_too_large`)

422.description: The document failed section validation; every failure is listed at once under `detail.errors`

403.description: The token lacks the scope this endpoint requires

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. The `Retry-After` response header gives the number of seconds to wait.

deck_id.title: Deck Id
deck_id.description: Target deck id (see GET /decks); omit to use the deck for the card's submission. When given, it must belong to that submission.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: vocab = from your vocabulary; custom = freeform front/back

pos.title: Pos
pos.description: Part of speech, to disambiguate (kind=vocab); case-insensitive

submission_id.title: Submission Id
submission_id.description: Disambiguate the lemma to one submission (kind=vocab); the source submission the card anchors to (REQUIRED for kind=custom). The card is deleted if that submission is later deleted.

sentence_position.title: Sentence Position
sentence_position.description: Optional 1-based transcript position (see GET /transcript) to anchor a custom card to a specific sentence, so the exported card shows that sentence and its native audio. Defaults to the submission's first sentence (kind=custom).

AddCardRequest.title: AddCardRequest
AddCardRequest.description:
Add a card to the learner's SRS (their system of record for word knowledge).

``kind=vocab`` adds a word the learner already has in their vocabulary,
resolved by ``lemma`` across their accessible content (pass ``submission_id``
to disambiguate a lemma that appears in several episodes, or ``pos`` when it
appears under several parts of speech). ``kind=custom`` adds a freeform
front/back card anchored to a submission.

Omit ``deck_id`` to add to the deck for the card's own submission - the same
deck the app builds and reviews from - so the card is immediately visible,
reviewable and exportable. Pass ``deck_id`` (from GET /decks) to target a
specific deck; it must be one of yours and must belong to the same
submission the card anchors to.

char_start.description: Span start as a code-point offset into the sentence display; null (with char_end) for a whole-sentence note

char_end.title: Char End
char_end.description: Span end (exclusive) code-point offset; null for a whole-sentence note

selected_text.title: Selected Text
selected_text.description: The server's snapshot of display[char_start:char_end] at create time. VERIFY this equals the span you intended; delete + recreate if it drifted (UTF-16 vs code-point offset mismatch). Null for a whole-sentence note.

stale.title: Stale
stale.description: True when the sentence was edited after the note was made, so the stored selected_text no longer matches the display slice; the app hides the tint but keeps the note

AnnotationV1.title: AnnotationV1
AnnotationV1.description:
A creator note anchored to a transcript sentence (a span, or the whole
sentence). Owners see it as a tint + note; followers get it as a card.

AudioSlice.title: AudioSlice
AudioSlice.description:
A [start, end) window into the source submission's ORIGINAL audio.

Playback is range-seek on the submission's own stream, exactly as Listen
plays sentences and loop ranges. No derived clip files exist anywhere in
the native lesson pipeline (no ffmpeg cutting, no stored bytes).

inf/nan are rejected: they pass ge/gt checks but canonical serialisation
emits them as null, which bricks every later re-read of the stored
document (a permanent 500 on the document/export routes).

id.title: Id
id.description: Server-managed stable identity; never author or modify it - the save route fills and preserves it.

listen_first.title: Listen First
listen_first.description: Start the original text layer blurred (paint-only, geometry stable); the learner reveals it with the existing layer control

show_words.title: Show Words
show_words.description: DEPRECATED, inert no-op (the reader is always-open now); use ``listen_first`` for anticipation rounds. Still accepted for back-compat

notes.title: Notes
notes.description: Numbered span-anchored footnotes below the reader passage

AudioSliceBlock.title: AudioSliceBlock
AudioSliceBlock.description:
A standalone player row (e.g. the whole-passage first listen).

``listen_first`` starts the passage's original text layer BLURRED
(paint-only, geometry stable - the layer sits in place at full size, only
the ink is washed), so the learner hears the passage before reading it and
reveals the text with the existing layer control when ready. This is the
anticipation / listen-first round: nothing is hidden from the DOM, so
reveal is instant and the layout never reflows.

``show_words`` is DEPRECATED and inert (a documented no-op): it once
rendered the live transcript words under the row, but the reader became
always-open, so the field no longer changes anything. It is still accepted
for backwards compatibility (documents authored while it was live must keep
validating). For a listen-first / anticipation round use ``listen_first``
instead; ``false`` here is NOT that (it is the schema default, so re-honouring
it would silently blur every older document that never opted in).

``notes`` is the numbered footnote apparatus below the passage (the
Assimil treatment the dialogue block carries), span-anchored into the
live transcript; see ``SliceNote``.

url.title: Url
url.description: Presigned URL for the full audio file

AudioUrlV1.title: AudioUrlV1
AudioUrlV1.description: A short-lived direct URL to the native audio (supports HTTP Range).

title.title: Title
title.description: Submission title; defaults to the audio filename

collection.title: Collection
collection.description: Slug of a collection you own to publish the submission to

notify.title: Notify
notify.description: 'true' to email you when processing finishes (opt-in)

sentence_position.title: Sentence Position
sentence_position.description: 1-based transcript position (see GET /transcript) of the example sentence the card anchors to.

focus_span.title: Focus Span
focus_span.description: VERBATIM substring of that sentence (the exact surface form, not the dictionary form: 'habe', not 'haben'). Drives the highlight/blur painting and, when word timings exist, the native audio clip of just this span. REQUIRED for grammar/cloze/collocation/contrast/production (it is the hidden answer); optional for the lexical kinds and qa.

CardExampleV1.title: CardExampleV1
CardExampleV1.description:
The transcript anchor for a card.v1 card. The sentence supplies the
example text, its translation, and its native audio; the focus span locates
the target inside it.

code.title: Code

detail.title: Detail
detail.description: What happened and how to fix it

CardProblemV1.title: CardProblemV1
CardProblemV1.description:
One non-fatal issue the server auto-corrected or degraded while saving a
card.v1 card. Returned so a generating agent can fix its input and resend.

problems.title: Problems

format.title: Format

deck_id.title: Deck Id
deck_id.description: Target deck id (see GET /decks); omit to use the deck for the card's submission. When given, it must belong to that submission.

submission_id.title: Submission Id
submission_id.description: The source submission the card anchors to. The card is deleted if that submission is later deleted.

kind.description: Card kind; see the model description.

headword.title: Headword
headword.description: Citation form shown on the card (include the article for gendered nouns: 'die Landschaft'). For qa this is the question.

translation.title: Translation
translation.description: Learner-language gloss (lexical kinds), the answer (qa), or the meaning prompt (production).

note.title: Note
note.description: One-line 'why' shown on the back note rail.

hint.title: Hint
hint.description: Optional on-demand hint (stored; surfaced by clients that support hint reveal).

cefr.title: Cefr

context_positions.title: Context Positions

correct.description: Contrast only: the right option; must be one of options.

question.description: qa only: overrides headword as the displayed question.

direction.title: Direction
direction.description: Lexical kinds only: 'both' (default, forward + reverse pair) or 'forward'. Study kinds are always forward-only.

CardV1Request.title: CardV1Request
CardV1Request.description:
Create a fully-featured card from declarative anchors (format card.v1).

The caller supplies WHAT to teach (kind, headword, translation, note) and
WHERE it lives in the recording (sentence position + verbatim focus span);
the server derives everything that makes native cards look good: the
per-word highlight/blur map, the native-audio clip of the focus span, and
the forward+reverse pair for lexical kinds. Anchors are verified against
the stored transcript - a paraphrased example is rejected, never saved.

Kinds: word | phrase | collocation | idiom | chunk (lexical; forward +
reverse pair, the headword is shown as a vocab-style entry) and grammar |
cloze | contrast | qa | production (study kinds; forward-only, rendered
with a type chip and per-kind chrome). For grammar-family kinds the focus
span is the answer and is blurred in the example.

CollectionListV1.title: CollectionListV1
CollectionListV1.description: The collections the caller may publish submissions to.

CollectionRefV1.title: CollectionRefV1
CollectionRefV1.description: The collection a piece of content belongs to.

CollectionV1.title: CollectionV1
CollectionV1.description: A collection the caller may publish submissions to.

CourseCreateRequest.title: CourseCreateRequest
CourseCreateRequest.description:
Create a course: an ordered series to file lessons under.

Courses are authored via the API / MCP only (there is no in-app course
editor). After creating one, save lessons into it with the create-lesson
request's ``course_id`` (+ optional ``sequence``).

lesson_count.title: Lesson Count

CourseV1.description: A course the caller owns, plus how many lessons are filed under it.

char_start.title: Char Start
char_start.description: Span start code-point offset into display

CreateAnnotationV1.title: CreateAnnotationV1
CreateAnnotationV1.description:
Create a creator annotation on a sentence span (or the whole sentence).

``char_start``/``char_end`` are both present for a span note and both null
for a whole-sentence note (enforced server-side). Offsets are UNICODE
CODE-POINT offsets into the sentence ``display`` from GET /transcript.
``selected_text`` is never accepted from the client - the server snapshots
the display slice and echoes it back so you can verify the span.

poll.title: Poll

DeckExportStartV1.title: DeckExportStartV1
DeckExportStartV1.description: Result of triggering a deck export.

DeckExportStatusV1.title: DeckExportStatusV1
DeckExportStatusV1.description: Current state of a deck's Anki export.

DeckListV1.title: DeckListV1
DeckListV1.description: The learner's decks, newest activity first.

id.description: Deck id (use as deck_id when adding/exporting)

source_submission_title.title: Source Submission Title
source_submission_title.description: Title of the submission this deck was auto-created from, if any

new_cards.title: New Cards
new_cards.description: Cards still in the FSRS 'new' state

DeckSummaryV1.title: DeckSummaryV1
DeckSummaryV1.description:
One of the learner's study decks, for discovering deck ids to add to or
export.

DeleteAnnotationResultV1.title: DeleteAnnotationResultV1
DeleteAnnotationResultV1.description: Result of deleting a creator annotation.

deleted_sentences.title: Deleted Sentences
deleted_sentences.description: How many draft rows were removed

DeleteDraftResultV1.title: DeleteDraftResultV1
DeleteDraftResultV1.description: Result of discarding a language's draft.

id.title: Id
id.description: Server-managed stable identity; never author or modify it - the save route fills and preserves it.

DialogueBlock.title: DialogueBlock
DialogueBlock.description:
The transcript slice as a dialogue.

``presentation`` selects the renderer treatment, presentation only with no
content semantics: ``auto`` (the default) preserves the current inference -
the Assimil coursebook treatment (facing translation column, literal gloss,
footnote apparatus) whenever any line carries a ``literal`` or a ``note``,
else the legacy speaker-forward layout; ``parallel`` forces the coursebook
treatment; ``conversation`` forces the speaker-forward treatment.

note.title: Note
note.description: Author's aside shown under the line

note_quote.title: Note Quote
note_quote.description: Exact substring of ``text`` the note is about; the renderer washes it and anchors the note's circled number at its end

example.title: Example
example.description: A fresh example sentence illustrating the note (target text)

example_translation.title: Example Translation
example_translation.description: Translation of ``example``, shown as an indented second line

DialogueLine.title: DialogueLine
DialogueLine.description:
One transcript sentence presented as a dialogue line.

``position`` anchors the line to the stored sentence (the save route
verifies ``text`` matches it, so a lesson cannot misattribute content to
the recording). ``highlights`` are [start, end) CODE-POINT ranges into
``text`` for the amber pattern marks (both renderers treat them as code
points; supplementary-plane characters count as one). Ranges must not
overlap - both renderers would otherwise duplicate the overlapped text.

``note`` is a short authorial aside under the line ("register: informal",
"this is the idiom from the title") - the author's voice, never quoted
content, so it needs no congruence check. Renders inline mini-Markdown
marks (bold/italic/code) only, like ``grammar_box.merke``.

``example``/``example_translation`` are the Assimil note shape: a fresh
example sentence with its translation, rendered as an indented second line
under the note. They belong to the note apparatus (author's content, not
quoted from the transcript, so no congruence check), which is why
``example`` requires ``note`` - it extends the note, it does not stand
alone - and ``example_translation`` requires ``example``.

id.title: Id
id.description: Server-managed stable identity; never author or modify it - the save route fills and preserves it.

DictationItem.title: DictationItem
DictationItem.description:
One sentence the learner transcribes by ear.

``position`` (REQUIRED) anchors the item to the stored sentence: the
renderer plays that sentence and diffs the learner's typing against its
LIVE transcript text (``display``/``text``), so the answer stays truthful
as the transcript is edited - nothing is copied into the document.

``audio`` is an OPTIONAL wider play window (e.g. to include a beat of
lead-in). By convention the author keeps it COVERING the sentence, since
the diff target is always the whole stored sentence, never the window; this
is an authoring expectation, not a validated invariant (the route only
bounds the window to the episode/slice, to avoid diff-time coupling to
sentence offsets). When absent the renderer plays the sentence's own
range.

translation.title: Translation
translation.description: Whole-sentence target text, or null for no sentence line

meanings.title: Meanings
meanings.description: One meaning per token, same order and length as the source sentence's tokens; '' for punctuation/interjections

DraftSentenceV1.title: DraftSentenceV1
DraftSentenceV1.description: One drafted sentence the agent submits (whole-sentence + per-token).

errors.title: Errors
errors.description: Every validation failure found at once

unknown_lemmas.title: Unknown Lemmas
unknown_lemmas.description: Advisory: vocab/review lemmas the sibling does not know

EditionRejectedV1.title: EditionRejectedV1
EditionRejectedV1.description:
The 422 body (under ``detail``) when a translated edition fails
reference validation against the sibling submission - every failure at
once, dry-run style.

code.title: Code
code.description: Stable machine-readable error code, present when the failure has one a client may branch on (e.g. ambiguous_lemma, duplicate_card, lesson_cap). Absent for errors without a specific code.

ErrorV1.title: ErrorV1
ErrorV1.description: The uniform error body for every /api/v1 failure.

ExerciseDictationBlock.title: ExerciseDictationBlock
ExerciseDictationBlock.description: Diktat: listen and type the sentence, then see a word-level diff.

ExerciseGapFillBlock.title: ExerciseGapFillBlock
ExerciseGapFillBlock.description:
Gap-fill, optionally cued by a word bank.

``style="dots"`` asks renderers for the Assimil cloze treatment: each
gap drawn as one dot per character of its first accepted answer, so
the shape of the missing word is itself a cue. Presentation-only.

ExerciseMatchBlock.description: Ordne zu: match words to meanings.

ExerciseMcqBlock.title: ExerciseMcqBlock
ExerciseMcqBlock.description:
Multiple choice (recognition or listening comprehension when ``audio``
is present).

ExerciseOrderBlock.title: ExerciseOrderBlock
ExerciseOrderBlock.description: Satzbau: reorder the scrambled chunks into the correct sentence.

id.description: Server-managed stable identity; never author or modify it - the save route fills and preserves it.

ExerciseProductionBlock.title: ExerciseProductionBlock
ExerciseProductionBlock.description: Free production on ruled lines with a blurred model answer.

id.title: Id
id.description: Server-managed stable identity; never author or modify it - the save route fills and preserves it.

ExerciseShadowBlock.title: ExerciseShadowBlock
ExerciseShadowBlock.description:
Shadowing: listen to the native line, record yourself, replay your take.

The product's core practice move, inline in a lesson. No waveform, no
scoring, no upload - just the loop. Positions join the existing reference
checks via ``sentence_positions()``; there is no new route validation and
no stored answer.

id.title: Id
id.description: Server-managed stable identity; never author or modify it - the save route fills and preserves it.

ExerciseTranslateBlock.title: ExerciseTranslateBlock
ExerciseTranslateBlock.description:
Translate/reconstruct: the Assimil daily translate exercise and the
active-wave reconstruction in one block (the interaction is identical - cue
in one language, produce the other, compare with a model, replay the source;
only direction, response mode and scheduling differ).

SCORING CONTRACT (stated here verbatim so every renderer and the export stay
in sync): an item contributes 1 scored point IFF direction is
``meaning_to_target`` AND response involves typing (``type`` or
``speak_then_type``) AND the item is anchored (``position`` set) - the
diffable-exact case, first-check all-or-nothing like dictation. Every other
configuration is self-assessed and contributes 0. Offline export never
scores this block (the anchored answer is live text and is never copied into
the artifact).

difficulty.title: Difficulty
difficulty.description: FSRS difficulty of the most advanced card (0-10 scale)

FsrsSummaryV1.title: FsrsSummaryV1
FsrsSummaryV1.description:
A learner's spaced-repetition state for one word, aggregated across all
the cards they hold for it (a word can have several card variants).

GapFillItem.title: GapFillItem
GapFillItem.description:
One sentence with {{n}} gap tokens; ``answers[n-1]`` lists the accepted
alternatives for gap n.

id.title: Id

GrammarBoxBlock.title: GrammarBoxBlock
GrammarBoxBlock.description:
The one-point-per-lesson rule box, wearing the Ask AI callout grammar:
evidence rows (the breakdown), a Merke takeaway, an Achtung watch-out.

``explanation`` renders the full mini-Markdown subset (see ``ProseBlock``);
``merke`` and ``achtung`` render inline marks (bold/italic/code) only.

GrammarEvidence.title: GrammarEvidence
GrammarEvidence.description: One evidence row: a piece of the actual passage plus why it matters.

GuidedActiveJobV1.title: GuidedActiveJobV1
GuidedActiveJobV1.description:
Live progress of the newest in-flight guided job (planner or writer).

Present only while a pass is running; poll it (or the returned job id via
/v1/jobs/{job_id}) to follow generation.

level.title: Level
level.description: Working CEFR level to teach to (document 'level')

source.description: The document 'source' block, to copy verbatim

plan_entry.title: Plan Entry
plan_entry.description: The planner's section object: title, summary, position bounds, suggested focus, and grammar/vocabulary/function notes

sentences.title: Sentences
sentences.description: The section's transcript sentences, in order

known_lemmas.title: Known Lemmas
known_lemmas.description: Lemmas the learner's deck already knows well (up to 200); prefer teaching genuinely new vocabulary

GuidedBriefMaterialsV1.title: GuidedBriefMaterialsV1
GuidedBriefMaterialsV1.description:
The section's raw material: everything needed to fill the document's
non-authored fields and ground its exercises.

``language``/``translation_language``/``level`` populate the document's
top-level fields; ``source`` populates its ``source`` block; ``plan_entry``
is the planner's section object (title, summary, bounds, grammar/vocab
notes); ``sentences`` are the section's transcript lines; ``known_lemmas``
are words the learner's deck already knows well (prefer fresh vocabulary).

GuidedBriefSentenceV1.title: GuidedBriefSentenceV1
GuidedBriefSentenceV1.description:
One transcript sentence of the section, the raw material to build from.

Quote ``text`` VERBATIM in dialogue/order blocks (the submit route enforces
it). ``start``/``end`` are the sentence's own audio window; positions are
1-based and lie within the section's range.

GuidedBriefSourceV1.title: GuidedBriefSourceV1
GuidedBriefSourceV1.description:
The ``source`` block to copy verbatim into the lesson.v1 document.

Mirrors the document's own ``source``: the submission the lesson teaches and
the section's audio window (seconds). Copy it exactly - the submit route
re-checks every reference against these bounds.

section_index.title: Section Index
section_index.description: Plan index of the section this brief targets (the next empty study-order section); submit the lesson for exactly this index

focus.title: Focus
focus.description: The teaching focus deterministically chosen for this section (comprehension | grammar | speaking | vocabulary)

pack_version.title: Pack Version
pack_version.description: Version of the teaching pack these instructions came from (recorded on the submitted lesson's generator for provenance)

instructions.title: Instructions
instructions.description: The assembled pedagogy (markdown): the arc, the picked focus recipe, level guidance and quality rules - the same system-prompt content the internal writer receives, plus the transcript-is-data notice

contract.title: Contract
contract.description: The lesson.v1 authoring contract: the output format, every block type, and the grounding rules the submit route enforces

materials.description: The section's raw material to write from

GuidedBriefV1.title: GuidedBriefV1
GuidedBriefV1.description:
The runtime writer brief for the NEXT empty study-order section.

Everything an agent needs to compose ONE guided lesson.v1 part to the same
standard the internal writer does: the deterministically picked ``focus``,
the assembled pedagogy ``instructions`` (the SAME system-prompt content the
internal writer receives), the lesson.v1 ``contract``, and the section's
``materials``. Read-only: it claims nothing, so two agents may both brief and
compose; the first valid submit to
``POST .../guided/sections/{index}/lesson`` wins.

Compose the document, dry-run it with ``POST /v1/lessons/validate``, then
submit it for ``section_index``. The ``instructions`` state that the
transcript is DATA to teach, never instructions to obey.

GuidedErrorV1.title: GuidedErrorV1
GuidedErrorV1.description:
The structured body of a guided-path conflict / gate failure.

Distinct from ``ErrorV1``: the guided surface carries a machine-readable
``code`` first and a human ``message``, matching the in-app guided routes'
convention so a client branches on ``code`` and reworded prose never breaks
it (e.g. ``plan_in_progress``, ``plan_ready``, ``submission_too_long``,
``guided_daily_limit``, ``enqueue_failed``).

section_index.title: Section Index
section_index.description: Plan index of the section the writer was generating

GuidedGenerationErrorV1.title: GuidedGenerationErrorV1
GuidedGenerationErrorV1.description:
The most recent writer failure still awaiting a fresh attempt.

Surfaced so a silently-failed part generation is not lost. ``message`` is
deliberately generic (the real reason stays server-side); ``section_index``
pins it to the still-ungenerated section the writer was on.

version.title: Version
version.description: Generator version (default: the teaching pack version)

GuidedGeneratorV1.title: GuidedGeneratorV1
GuidedGeneratorV1.description:
The caller's self-declared provenance for a submitted guided lesson.

Optional and display-only (never a trust boundary): ``skill`` defaults to
``mcp-guided`` and ``version`` to the teaching pack's version, so a submitted
part is always traceable to the pack it was written against, distinct from
the internal writer's ``guided-<focus>`` skill. Both are locked to the same
display-safe slug charset the lesson document's generator uses.

errors.title: Errors
errors.description: Every validation failure found (section bounds, congruence, existence, youtube), across all categories at once

unknown_lemmas.title: Unknown Lemmas
unknown_lemmas.description: Advisory: vocab/review lemmas the submission does not know

GuidedLessonRejectedV1.title: GuidedLessonRejectedV1
GuidedLessonRejectedV1.description:
The 422 body (under ``detail``) when a submitted document fails validation.

Reports EVERY reference/section failure at once (the dry-run validate style),
so an agent can fix the whole document in one pass rather than one save cycle
per error. Schema-level failures never reach this: they are rejected as the
request's own 422 by the document model.

GuidedLessonSavedV1.description:

GuidedLessonSubmitRequest.title: GuidedLessonSubmitRequest
GuidedLessonSubmitRequest.description:
Submit a composed lesson.v1 ``document`` as the next guided part.

The document is re-validated against the target section (positions in-section,
dialogue verbatim, audio inside the section's window; youtube blocks rejected)
and attached atomically. ``generator`` is optional self-declared provenance;
the server stamps it (defaulting skill to ``mcp-guided`` and version to the
pack version) onto the stored document, overriding any generator the document
itself carries.

course_id.description: Id of the course the generated lessons are filed under; null until the first part is written

generating_section.title: Generating Section

LessonUpdateRequest.description:

The whole document is submitted and re-validated exactly like a save

stored object. The lesson keeps its id, visibility, course and creation
time - only the document (and the metadata denormalised from it) changes.
The document must keep its original ``source.submission_id``: re-pointing
a lesson at a different episode is a new lesson, not an edit. HTML lessons
are not updatable (they have no document).

LessonValidateRequest.title: LessonValidateRequest
LessonValidateRequest.description:
Dry-run validate a lesson.v1 ``document`` without saving it.

The body mirrors the save request's ``document`` field, but the document is
validated leniently (as raw JSON) so EVERY schema failure can be reported in
the 200 response rather than surfacing as a single 422. Only lesson.v1
documents are validatable; an ``html`` body (the legacy save variant) is
rejected, since HTML lessons have no structured invariants to check.

loc.title: Loc
loc.description: Dotted path into the document (schema_invalid failures only)

positions.description: Every affected 1-based sentence position (position-anchored reference failures)

audio_windows.description: Every offending [start, end] audio window (audio reference failures)

LessonValidationErrorV1.title: LessonValidationErrorV1
LessonValidationErrorV1.description:
One validation failure found in a dry run.

``code`` is the stable category. Schema failures carry a ``loc`` (a dotted
path into the document, e.g. ``blocks.3.lines.0.position``) and the pydantic
``message``. Reference/gate failures carry the same stable codes the save
route raises (``youtube_disabled``, ``unknown_positions``,
``position_outside_slice``, ``audio_outside_episode``, ``audio_outside_slice``,
``dialogue_mismatch``, ``order_mismatch``) with the offending ``positions``
or ``audio_windows`` spelled out in full (the human ``message`` truncates
long lists; these do not).

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description:
One sentence to reassemble from scrambled chunks.

``segments`` are the tokens/chunks IN CORRECT ORDER (the renderer
scrambles them for the learner). When ``position`` anchors the item to a
stored sentence, the save route checks the segments reassemble it under a
whitespace-insensitive comparison, so a reorder exercise cannot drift from
the recording (``strip_all_whitespace``).

id.title: Id
id.description: Server-managed stable identity; never author or modify it - the save route fills and preserves it.

ProseBlock.title: ProseBlock
ProseBlock.description:
Instruction lines and short passages.

``text`` renders through the app's mini-Markdown subset: ``**bold**``,
``*italic*``, backtick code, ``- ``/``* `` bullet lines, and blank-line
paragraph breaks. Headings, links, tables and raw HTML are NOT rendered -
they show as literal text. The same subset applies to
``grammar_box.explanation``; exercise ``instruction`` fields and
``grammar_box.merke``/``achtung`` render the inline marks only. All other
text fields (titles, prompts, options, meanings, dialogue) are plain text.

``style="culture"`` renders as a lightly ruled labelled callout (the Assimil
``nota culturale`` treatment - a "Culture note" caption, ink-bordered like
``grammar_box`` but lighter), giving a cultural aside its own visual
identity as a different kind of text. It uses the same mini-Markdown rules
as ``body``; the difference is presentation only, no content semantics.

generator.title: Generator

SectionBlock.description: A numbered section header; the renderer assigns the numbers.

SentenceSearchHitV1.description: A sentence matching a search, with its source and timestamps.

SentenceSearchV1.title: SentenceSearchV1

id.title: Id
id.description: Server-managed stable identity; never author or modify it - the save route fills and preserves it.

ShadowItem.title: ShadowItem
ShadowItem.description:
One sentence the learner shadows (listen → record → replay their take).

``position`` (REQUIRED) anchors the item to the stored sentence: the
renderer plays that sentence's own range from the LIVE transcript and shows
its live text, so the line stays truthful as the transcript is edited -
nothing is copied into the document. The recorded take is in-memory only
(never uploaded, never stored), so there is no answer to check or leak and
the block contributes nothing to the score (self-assessed, like
production).

quote.title: Quote
quote.description: Exact substring of the live sentence the note is about

example.title: Example
example.description: A fresh example sentence illustrating the note (target text)

example_translation.title: Example Translation
example_translation.description: Translation of ``example``, shown as an indented second line

SliceNote.title: SliceNote
SliceNote.description:
A numbered footnote under an ``audio_slice`` reader passage.

``position`` anchors the note to a live transcript sentence; ``quote``
names the exact substring of that sentence the note is about. Matching is
RENDER-TIME only (nothing is copied into the document, the same live-text
rule as ``show_words``): the renderer washes the found span and sets the
circled note number at its end, and a quote the live text no longer
contains degrades to a sentence-end marker, so a transcript edit can
never paint a wrong span. ``text`` renders inline mini-Markdown marks
(bold/italic/code) only, like ``DialogueLine.note``.

``example``/``example_translation`` are the Assimil note shape: a fresh
example sentence with its translation, rendered as an indented second line
under the note. They are the author's content (not quoted from the
transcript), so they carry no congruence check; only the pairing rule
applies - ``example_translation`` requires ``example`` (a translation with
nothing to translate is meaningless). The note itself always has ``text``,
so ``example`` needs no separate anchor of its own.

Distinguishes a genuinely empty slice from 'not done yet'.

TranslateItem.title: TranslateItem
TranslateItem.description:
One translate prompt, in one of two shapes.

ANCHORED (``position`` set): the item is a live transcript sentence. Nothing
is copied into the document - the live sentence supplies the target text
(in ``meaning_to_target``, diffed like dictation) or the cue
(``target_to_meaning``), and the stored sentence translation is the fallback
model answer / cue. ``cue`` and ``model_answer`` here are author OVERRIDES
only (used when the author wants wording other than the live text or its
stored translation), never required; ``source_positions`` must be empty
because the anchor sentence IS the source, so naming other sources would be
contradictory.

RECOMBINATION (``position`` unset): the item is a fresh sentence that
recombines the lesson's material, so it is NOT in the transcript. It must
carry its own ``cue`` AND ``model_answer`` (there is no live text to fall
back on) and at least one ``source_positions`` entry naming the sentences
its chunks came from, so an invented sentence stays grounded and lintable.

``model_answer`` sits in Pydantic's protected ``model_`` namespace, opened
here as it is on ``ExerciseProductionBlock``.

pivot_translation.description: The whole-sentence translation in the pivot language

tokens.title: Tokens
tokens.description: Tokens in order; one meaning per token, same length, is required

TranslationSourceSentenceV1.title: TranslationSourceSentenceV1
TranslationSourceSentenceV1.description: One source sentence with its pivot translation and tokens to gloss.

surface.title: Surface
surface.description: The token as it appears in the sentence

pivot_meaning.title: Pivot Meaning
pivot_meaning.description: The word's meaning in the pivot language; fixes the sense the agent must render (do not re-interpret it)

TranslationSourceTokenV1.title: TranslationSourceTokenV1
TranslationSourceTokenV1.description: One token the agent must gloss, with its resolved pivot sense.

pivot_language.title: Pivot Language
pivot_language.description: Language the primary is glossed in

next_from_position.title: Next From Position
next_from_position.description: Pass as from_position for the next page; null when exhausted

TranslationSourceV1.title: TranslationSourceV1
TranslationSourceV1.description: A page of source sentences for the agent to translate.

path.description: The unit path exactly as served by the translation source

text.title: Text
text.description: The translated (or passed-through) text for this unit

TranslationUnitPutV1.title: TranslationUnitPutV1
TranslationUnitPutV1.description: One translated unit sent back: the source unit's path plus your text.

path.title: Path
path.description: Dotted path addressing this string in the source (e.g. 'blocks.7.options.2')

text.title: Text
text.description: The source text to translate or adapt

max_length.title: Max Length
max_length.description: Character cap the translated text must respect

passthrough_if_target.title: Passthrough If Target
passthrough_if_target.description: True when the text may legitimately already be in the target language and must then be returned unchanged

context.title: Context
context.description: Owning model and field (e.g. 'DialogueLine.literal'), for per-field guidance

TranslationUnitV1.title: TranslationUnitV1
TranslationUnitV1.description:
One translatable meta-language string of a lesson document or guided
plan, addressed by its dotted path.

``kind``: ``render`` = translate faithfully; ``adapt`` = localise for the
new learner language (contrastive content: grammar explanations,
watch-outs, literal glosses, notes, translate cues). One rule binds every
unit regardless of kind: text already in the TARGET language must be
returned unchanged (B1+ lessons write instructions, B2+ everything, in
the target language by design). ``passthrough_if_target`` marks the
fields where that ambiguity is structural at any level (MCQ
prompt/options).

missing_paths.title: Missing Paths
missing_paths.description: Source unit paths absent from the payload (first 20)

unknown_paths.title: Unknown Paths
unknown_paths.description: Payload paths the source does not contain (first 20)

TranslationUnitsErrorV1.title: TranslationUnitsErrorV1
TranslationUnitsErrorV1.description:
The 400 body when the submitted units do not fit the source.

``unit_coverage``: the map must cover the source's units EXACTLY -
``missing_paths``/``unknown_paths`` name the difference (samples, capped
at 20 each). ``invalid_units``: per-unit value failures in ``problems``.
Fix everything in ONE retry; all violations are reported at once.

UnitProblemV1.title: UnitProblemV1
UnitProblemV1.description:
One rejected unit value (code: unit_empty, unit_too_long,
unit_collapsed).

UpdateAnnotationV1.title: UpdateAnnotationV1
UpdateAnnotationV1.description: Replace an annotation's note (the only editable field).

id.title: Id
id.description: Server-managed stable identity; never author or modify it - the save route fills and preserves it.

forms.title: Forms
forms.description: Principal forms, e.g. 'zog um · ist umgezogen'

position.title: Position
position.description: Anchor sentence in the transcript, when known

show_sentence.title: Show Sentence
show_sentence.description: Render the anchor sentence (live) under the row

VocabEntry.title: VocabEntry
VocabEntry.description:
One glossary word. FSRS state (new/due/known) is NEVER stored here -
the renderer resolves it live so flags stay truthful as the lesson ages.

``show_sentence`` renders the anchor sentence's LIVE transcript text under
the row (the word in its real context), so it requires ``position``. Like
dictation/shadow lines, nothing is copied into the document - the quote
stays truthful as the transcript is edited, and the offline export omits
it (live text is never copied into artifacts).

pos.title: Pos
pos.description: Part of speech, e.g. NOUN, VERB, ADJ; an empty string when the source card has no part of speech recorded

translation.title: Translation
translation.description: Meaning in the learner's translation language

reading.title: Reading
reading.description: Phonetic reading (pinyin/furigana). Always null in v1; reserved for a future non-breaking fill.

card_count.title: Card Count
card_count.description: How many cards the user holds for this word

source_submission_id.title: Source Submission Id
source_submission_id.description: A submission this word was learned from (the most advanced card)

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: When any of the word's cards last changed; use as the 'since' value for the next incremental sync

VocabularyEntryV1.title: VocabularyEntryV1
VocabularyEntryV1.description:
One word in the learner's vocabulary, grounded in real listening content.

Aggregated per (lemma, part-of-speech, language): the FSRS block summarises
every card the user holds for the word, while the dictionary fields come
from the enrichment of the source submission.

next_cursor.title: Next Cursor
next_cursor.description: Pass as 'cursor' to fetch the next page; null when no more

VocabularyListV1.title: VocabularyListV1
VocabularyListV1.description: A page of vocabulary entries with an opaque continuation cursor.

pos.title: Pos
pos.description: Part of speech of the representative match (most advanced FSRS state, then card count, then alphabetical)

pos_variants.title: Pos Variants
pos_variants.description: Other parts of speech this lemma also appears under in the user's cards (the representative `pos` excluded), so nothing is hidden

translation.title: Translation
translation.description: Meaning, from the learner's own content if present

reading.title: Reading
reading.description: Phonetic reading (pinyin/furigana). Always null in v1; reserved for a future non-breaking fill.

fsrs.description: Spaced-repetition state, present only when the user has cards for this word

card_count.title: Card Count
card_count.description: How many cards the user holds for this word

source_submission_id.title: Source Submission Id
source_submission_id.description: A submission the word was learned from, if any

lexicon_source.title: Lexicon Source
lexicon_source.description: Source of the fallback gender/CEFR when the shared lexicon supplied them (e.g. 'llm', 'wiktionary')

lexicon_confidence.title: Lexicon Confidence
lexicon_confidence.description: Confidence of the shared-lexicon fallback, if used

VocabularyLookupV1.title: VocabularyLookupV1
VocabularyLookupV1.description:
A single-word lookup: the learner's own context if present, backed by the
shared enrichment lexicon as a fallback for gender/CEFR.

YoutubeSlice.title: YoutubeSlice
YoutubeSlice.description:
A [start, end) window into a YouTube video (feature-flagged).

Mirrors ``AudioSlice`` but names a YouTube video instead of a range into
the submission's own stream. ``video_id`` is the bare 11-character id (the
renderer/export build the nocookie embed and watch URLs from it, never a
caller-supplied URL, so only the locked id charset ever reaches an href or
iframe src). ``start``/``end`` are OPTIONAL: absent means play the whole
video; when both are present ``end`` must be greater than ``start``.

inf/nan are rejected for the same reason as ``AudioSlice``: they pass ge
checks but canonical serialisation emits them as null, bricking every later
re-read of the stored document.

id.title: Id
id.description: Server-managed stable identity; never author or modify it - the save route fills and preserves it.

YoutubeSliceBlock.title: YoutubeSliceBlock
YoutubeSliceBlock.description:
A YouTube player row (feature-flagged; see ``settings.youtube_enabled``).

The video counterpart of ``AudioSliceBlock``: it plays a YouTube embed
instead of the submission's own audio. Only valid on deployments with the
YouTube integration enabled, so the save/validate routes reject a document
carrying this block while the flag is off (flag-off behaviour is unchanged,
no YouTube surface can be stored). The offline HTML export renders it as a
plain "Watch on YouTube" link, since exported HTML lives outside the app and
carries no embedded player.

bearerAuth.description: Personal access token (prefix `lcp_`) created in account settings. Send as `Authorization: Bearer lcp_...`.
